key_risks: A hotter-than-expected June CPI/PPI or hawkish FOMC communication could lift yields and trigger a sharp growth-stock de-rating.
Recent extreme gains in solar, South Korea, semiconductors, and cybersecurity could reverse through profit-taking or factor rotation into value/defensives.
Any disappointment in AI capex expectations, semiconductor demand, or large-cap tech guidance could disproportionately hurt the portfolio.
Geopolitical or trade tensions in East Asia could pressure South Korea and semiconductor supply-chain exposures.
A broader risk-off move from weak labor or consumer data could cause high-beta thematic ETFs to underperform the S&P 500.

portfolio.rationale: Strong recent relative momentum with direct support from large AI spending forecasts and ongoing AI infrastructure capex narrative likely to remain price-relevant over the month.
Semiconductor sales growth and AI compute demand provide near-term fundamental support, while the group has sustained strong momentum versus broad equities.
Cybersecurity has the strongest 30-day trailing return among liquid thematic tech options and is supported by forecast growth in AI-related security spending.
Exceptional short- and medium-term momentum with semiconductor/export sensitivity offers high beta to the prevailing AI and electronics risk-on trend.
Very strong recent momentum and clean-energy sentiment can continue over a one-month window, with EIA solar generation forecasts providing a supportive narrative.

portfolio_rationale: The portfolio is intentionally concentrated in high-momentum AI, semiconductor, cybersecurity, and related risk-on exposures because the scoring horizon is only one month and recent trend leadership is pronounced. It avoids low-beta bonds and defensive sectors because tight credit spreads, low volatility, and still-positive growth data favor continued equity risk appetite despite inflation uncertainty.
rationale_summary: A concentrated momentum and AI-linked allocation offers the best chance to outperform the S&P 500 over the June window. The main premise is trend persistence in technology and selected high-beta international/thematic exposures before the June 29 exit.
